task_completion.description: Open the draw.io application to create the diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io (diagrams.net) application interface is visible on the screen with a blank or partially complete canvas.

task_completion.description: Create a system boundary containing use cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large rectangular container labeled 'SecureHub System' containing at least 5 oval shapes (use cases) with text such as 'Unlock Door', 'Arm/Disarm System', 'Authenticate User', etc.

task_completion.description: Place actors outside the system boundary
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 3 stick-figure icons (actors) labeled with terms like 'Homeowner', 'Guest User', 'Emergency Services', or 'Cloud Backend' positioned outside the main system boundary box.

task_completion.description: Add <<include>> and <<extend>> relationships between use cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dashed arrows connecting use case ovals. Look for the text '<<include>>' on arrows (e.g., pointing to 'Authenticate User') and '<<extend>>' on arrows (e.g., pointing from 'Notify Police' to 'Trigger Panic Alarm').

task_completion.description: Save the diagram as a .drawio file on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog showing the filename 'securehub_usecase.drawio' in the Desktop directory, the draw.io window title showing this filename, or the .drawio file icon appearing on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Export the diagram as a .png file to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io 'Export as -> PNG' menu being used, an export save dialog showing 'securehub_usecase.png', or the .png file icon appearing on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the draw.io GUI to build the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the mouse cursor interacting with the draw.io toolbars, dragging shapes onto the canvas, and typing text into the shapes, rather than using terminal scripts to generate the XML file.

integrity.description: Verify the diagram was built progressively
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram should appear in various stages of completion across multiple screenshots, proving it was not simply downloaded or copy-pasted as a finished file.

integrity.description: Verify the PNG was exported using the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the draw.io export functionality (File -> Export as -> PNG) rather than using terminal commands like 'touch' or 'convert' to fake the image file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are '/home/ga/Desktop/securehub_usecase.drawio' and '/home/ga/Desktop/securehub_usecase.png'. The diagram must contain at least 3 actors, at least 5 use cases, and both '<<include>>' and '<<extend>>' relationships. Required terms to look for in the diagram include: homeowner, guest, emergency, cloud, unlock, camera, panic, arm, disarm.
